arts.ttl: Lewis-Skelly, Burn handed debuts against Albania
arts.sum: Thomas Tuchel has handed debuts to Myles Lewis-Skelly and Dan Burn in his first game as England boss, with Marcus Rashford also named in the starting lineup to face Albania.

arts.sum: Kyle Walker starts in Thomas Tuchel’s first match, meaning Milan are the non-British club to...

arts.ttl: England v Albania: World Cup 2026 qualifier – live
arts.sum: World Cup qualifying news from the 7.45pm GMT kick-off Live scores | Read Football Daily | And you can email Scott Kyle Walker – who made his debut under Fabio Capello and has subsequently played for Roy Hodgson, Sam Allardyce, Gareth Southgate and Lee Carsley – talks to ITV. “It’s been good … it’s exciting … a new era … I fully enjoyed it under Gareth Southgate … many great memories … turn the page and start again … Tuchel wants a high-intensity game … energy … being smart as well … we don’t want it to be 6-6 … we want to win at the end of the day.” Thomas Tuchel begins his reign as England manager by handing out two new caps. Man-of-the-moment Dan Burn, a scorer at Wembley five days ago as Newcastle slaked their 70-year domestic thirst, and Arsenal prodigy Myles Lewis-Skelly are the debutants. Marcus Rashford makes his first start for England since facing Malta in November 2023. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Rashford, Burn and Lewis-Skelly start in Tuchel's first England XI
arts.sum: Marcus Rashford, Myles Lewis-Skelly and Dan Burn were all handed starts in Thomas Tuchel's first England starting lineup for Friday's World Cup qualifier against Albania.

arts.ttl: Non-League Day could offer fans the chance to see future England stars play
arts.sum: Non-league day takes place this weekend with the absence of Premier Leagu and Championship football providing ample opportunity for football fans to see teams further down the pyramid

arts.ttl: Schick hot on Kane's trail in hunt to be Bundesliga top-scorer
arts.sum: The 29-year-old marksman is hopeful he can finish the campaign as the Bundesrepublik's top-scorer, all while challenging Bayern for the Meisterschale.

arts.ttl: England’s Transfer XI: Tuchel’s best team based on summer signings
arts.sum: Spurs signed Archie Gray from Leeds for a massive €41.25 million in the summer despite him being just 18 years of age. Lewis Hall was a promising player on loan at Newcastle last season, but he didn't look particularly great.

arts.ttl: Ethan Nwaneri: Arsenal phenom receives World Cup approval despite Thomas Tuchel England snub
arts.sum: England boss Thomas Tuchel was right to snub Ethan Nwaneri, but the Arsenal starlet is a safe bet for the 2026 World Cup, expert Charles Watts claims.
